_about.purpose: Known-pairs safety net for AcademiBot's cross-source deduplication (merge_bot.py).

field_shapes.arxiv: id, title, authors[], summary, published(YYYY-MM-DD), abs_link, pdf_link  — NO doi, NO year
field_shapes.semantic: id, title, authors[], year, abstract, citations, url, pdf_url, arxiv_id  — NO top-level doi (unused in externalIds)
field_shapes.ieee: id, title, authors[], year, abstract, citations, doi, url, pdf_url, publication
field_shapes.openalex: id, title, authors[], year, abstract, citations, doi, url, pdf_url

_about.dedup_rule_under_test: Two papers are duplicates if (both DOIs present AND normalized-equal) OR (both titles present AND normalized-equal). DOI is checked first, title is the fallback.
_about.known_weakness: ArXiv has no DOI and Semantic Scholar does not extract one, so for those sources dedup relies on exact normalized-title equality. Two genuinely different papers that share a generic title collide, and a title match is NOT blocked even when both papers carry DIFFERENT DOIs.

should_dedupe.reason: Same paper: ArXiv preprint vs IEEE published version. ArXiv has no DOI, so DOI matching cannot help — this MUST match on normalized title.

a.title: Real-Time Loop Closure in 2D LIDAR SLAM
a.summary: We present a real-time loop closure method for 2D LIDAR SLAM using submaps and branch-and-bound scan matching.

b.title: Real-Time Loop Closure in 2D LIDAR SLAM
b.abstract: A real-time loop closure approach for 2D LIDAR SLAM.
b.publication: 2016 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)

should_dedupe.reason: Same paper across IEEE and OpenAlex, matched by DOI. DOI prefix differs (raw '10.1109/...' vs 'https://doi.org/10.1109/...'). Titles are deliberately worded differently so ONLY the DOI path can match — verifies DOI normalization.

a.title: Path Planning for Autonomous Vehicles in Urban Environments
a.abstract: Urban path planning for autonomous vehicles.
a.publication: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems

b.title: Motion Planning for Self-Driving Cars in the City
b.abstract: Planning for self-driving cars in city settings.

should_dedupe.reason: Same paper, no DOIs on either side. Title differs only in casing and punctuation ('SLAM: A Survey of Techniques' vs 'Slam - A Survey Of Techniques'). Must match after title normalization.
Same paper, identical title but author names formatted differently ('J. Smith' vs 'John Smith'). Dedup keys on title only, so this must still match.

a.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Robotic Manipulation

b.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Robotic Manipulation

should_dedupe.reason: Same paper across IEEE and OpenAlex, matched by DOI where only the CASE differs ('10.1109/ICRA...' vs '10.1109/icra...'). Verifies case-insensitive DOI normalization.

b.title: Visual Inertial Odometry with Tightly Coupled Fusion

should_dedupe.reason: EDGE — missing DOI on one side. ArXiv preprint (no DOI) vs OpenAlex record (has DOI) of the same paper; identical title carries the match.

a.title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows
a.summary: A hierarchical vision transformer using shifted windows.

b.title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows
b.abstract: Hierarchical vision transformer with shifted windows.

should_dedupe.reason: EDGE — missing abstract on one side, but DOIs match exactly. DOI path must dedupe regardless of the empty abstract.

a.title: A Benchmark for Point Cloud Registration

b.title: A Benchmark for Point-Cloud Registration
b.abstract: A benchmark dataset and protocol for registration.

should_not_dedupe.reason: Different papers with similar but NON-identical generic titles ('A Survey of Deep Learning' vs 'A Survey of Deep Learning for Natural Language Processing'). Different DOIs. Normalized titles differ, so current logic correctly keeps them separate.

b.title: A Survey of Deep Learning for Natural Language Processing
b.abstract: Survey of deep learning applied to NLP.

should_not_dedupe.reason: KNOWN FALSE POSITIVE — two genuinely different papers that share the exact generic title 'Deep Learning', with DIFFERENT DOIs and different authors/years. A differing DOI should BLOCK a title merge, but current logic falls back to the identical title and wrongly merges them. Expected: NOT duplicate.

b.title: Deep Learning
b.abstract: A different tutorial paper that happens to be titled 'Deep Learning'.

should_not_dedupe.reason: KNOWN FALSE POSITIVE (found via integration test) — two different papers sharing the generic title 'Deep Learning' where ONE has a DOI and the other does not (ArXiv preprint vs an unrelated IEEE paper). The DOI veto cannot fire (needs both DOIs), so only the secondary signal separates them: authors and years both differ. Expected: NOT duplicate.

b.title: Deep Learning
b.abstract: A different IEEE paper that happens to share the title.

should_not_dedupe.reason: Clearly different papers: different titles, no shared DOI. Baseline sanity check — must NOT dedupe.

a.title: Monocular Visual Odometry for Ground Vehicles

b.title: Stereo Depth Estimation with Cost Volume Filtering

should_not_dedupe.reason: KNOWN FALSE POSITIVE — two different papers sharing the exact title 'Introduction to Robotics', neither with a DOI (both ArXiv/S2 style). Current title-only logic wrongly merges them. Expected: NOT duplicate.

EDGE / KNOWN FALSE POSITIVE — identical title 'Neural Network Design' but two genuinely different works: different authors AND different publication years (2018 vs 2021), no DOIs. Under the year-OR-first-author policy this must stay distinct (both signals differ). Current (pre-fix) logic ignores year/author and merges on title. Expected: NOT duplicate.

b.title: Neural Network Design
b.abstract: A distinct 2021 work that happens to share the title.
